observations.recommendation: We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to trace the amount of discrepancy of P2,740,340.60 so correcting entry could be immediately made.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the General Services Officer to submit to the Audit Team for review and validation a Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to support the assertion that 5,263 bags of rice still remained at year end or, a Distribution List duly signed by the recipient beneficiaries. If both RPCI and Distribution List could not be submitted after ten days from receipt of this AOM, a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued by the Audit Team to the persons liable for the transaction.
observations.managementAction: The Distribution Lists were still not transmitted to the accounting office for recording.

observations.recommendation: For similar transactions in the future, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor should issue a clear instruction to an Officer who shall be responsible and accountable for the transaction to pinpoint accountability and ensure that government resources are safeguarded against wastage.
observations.managementAction: No specific/ key personnel responsible for specific transactions were identified.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare journal entries to record inventory issuances only on the basis of a duly accomplished RSMI with supporting RIS.
observations.managementAction: The RIS for inventories issued were already prepared upon issuance of the inventories.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should submit a copy of the Appropriation Ordinance authorizing the disbursements of P16,634,402.00 for COVID-19 programs out of the CY 2020 20% Development Fund of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Justifications were submitted by the Municipal Mayor to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Local Sanggunian and all department heads should strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 7160, Administrative Order No. 47 dated April 12, 1993 and PD 1445 on the budgetary requirements for the proper disposition of public funds.
observations.managementAction: The municipality was restricted to spend only for essential operating expenditures on CY 2021 due to re-enacted budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the GF and prepare all the required financial reports for the four (4) economic enterprise of the Municipality for monitoring of the operating performance and viability of the LEEs and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Transactions pertaining to an economic enterprise should be recorded in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the LEE Managers (personnel in-charge) prepare the required 5-year business plan for each LEE.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Finance Committee conduct the required Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review and submit and present the same to the Municipal Mayor and local sanggunian.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should discontinue designating Job Order personnel as Collectors for the reason that they are not eligible for a fidelity bond with the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all personnel in possession with funds and property owned by the Municipality should be a permanent employee and properly bonded in accordance with the provisions of the law.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended further that the BAC should examine thoroughly the data contained in the Performance Securities and all other bidding documents of the Contracts so that validity of documents attached to it can be assured of and avoid invalidation of the Contract Agreement.
observations.managementAction: There are still various procurements whose contracts entered into were not supported with valid supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: Conduct physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real properties within the Municipality are properly listed in the assessment rolls;
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Because the LGU lacks technical personnel to conduct the physical survey

observations.recommendation: Conduct vigorous Tax Mapping activities in order to resolve issues on duplicate Tax Declarations;
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Due to same reason above.

observations.recommendation: undertake appraisal and general revision of real property assessments every three (3) years as required in Section 219 of RA 7160 in order to come up with a reliable amount of assessment on real property taxes;
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The LGU is still waiting for the instruction from the Provincial Government for the schedule of General Revision.

observations.recommendation: update and reconcile all records pertaining to real properties within the Municipality and its owners.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Since updating & reconciliation will be done after physical survey & tax mapping

observations.recommendation: update the Taxpayer’s Index Card and reconcile the same with the records of the Municipal Assessors;
observations.managementAction: Updating of record is on-going
observations.reason: Same reason given for the 4th recommendation

observations.recommendation: submit such list to the Sangguniang Bayan on or before December 31 of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, and request for assistance from the SB in the enforcement of the remedies for collection provided in RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Assessor determine the cost of the road lot component for each road project of the LGU for recognition in the financial statements;
observations.managementAction: The implementation of the audit recommendation is still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials concerned impose liquidated damages on Toyota Tacloban, Leyte, Inc. in the amount of P107,726.60, pursuant to Item 3.1 of Annex “D” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No liquidated damages were imposed since the delay was due to the customization of the vehicle.

observations.recommendation: require the Local Finance Committee to review the operations of Palompon Eco-Adventure Park and render a report thereon; and
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No review was made yet and this is reiterated in Finding No. 9 of this report.

observations.recommendation: use the said report to evaluate the viability of said enterprise;
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Could not be implemented since no report was made.

observations.recommendation: create other revenue-generating activities to improve the Park’s income, otherwise, reduce the expense for wages of JOs.
observations.reason: Due to COVID-19

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the hiring for vacant Plantilla Positions, subject to the prescribed PS limitation.
observations.managementAction: Appointments were disapproved by SB in CY 2021 for various reasons stated upon disapproval and continuous appointment of those personnel by the Municipal Mayor despite disapproval of SB, resulted to non-filling out of vacant positions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Office to prepare and submit the required reports monthly to the Office of the COA Audit Team thru the Municipal Accountant, in accordance with regulations.
observations.managementAction: The implementation of the audit recommendation is still on-going.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Internal Audit Unit in the LGU to handle internal control functions within the agency.
observations.managementAction: Creation of the IAU was not prioritized due to limitations imposed on creation of plantilla items since the LGU is operating under a re-enacted budget.
observations.reason: The office was still not created as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents on the dormant receivable accounts with evidences that the same could no longer be collected.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The necessary documents are not yet complete.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for write-off based on the documents submitted by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has written-off the receivable account from the books even without the submission of the request for write-off.
observations.reason: The request for write-off could not be made since the supporting  documents for the request are not complete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the General Services Officer submit to the accounting office for recording the Distribution Lists duly signed by the recipient beneficiaries of the above-noted inventories and copy furnished the Audit Team of the reports for review and validation.
For similar transactions in the future, we recommended that the General Services Officer prepare the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) upon distribution/issuance of the stocks to the end users and summarize all RIS in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued on a monthly basis and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record all the transactions of the municipality using the proper accounts provided in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs so that transactions are faithfully and correctly presented in the financial statements which will provide reliable data to the users thereof.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers concerned submit within 10 days from receipt of the AOM, the liquidation reports and its supporting documents, otherwise, restitute/return immediately the amount equivalent to their respective unliquidated cash advances.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Demand from the accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances, the immediate settlement, refund or submission of liquidation reports on cash advances; and
Recommend to the Municipal Mayor the withholding of salaries of the accountable officers who continue to fail to submit their Liquidation Reports within the period prescribed by regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Issue a directive to the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of officials and employees who failed to liquidate their cash advances, based on the recommendations of the Municipal Accountant; and
exhaust all possible remedies to demand liquidation of cash advances for employees no longer connected with the municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer withhold the salaries of officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances, as directed by the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Head of Procuring Entity, submit explanation to the Audit Team why contracts were awarded despite absence of availability of funds.  For future similar transactions, ensure that award of contracts to suppliers shall only be made when supported with actual funds available.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM Council, through the MDRRM Officer, ensure that the PPAs in the MDRRM Fund Investment Plan are implemented according to their intended purpose and that the approved quantity and budget of the PPAs be duly considered upon procurement to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC), through the MDRRM Officer, include in the annual MDRRMF Investment Plan the programs, projects and activities to be funded from the prior years’ unexpended MDRRMF to ensure that its utilization are well-planned and duly authorized by the MDRRM Council.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an accounting entry to record the liquidation of the completed projects implemented out of YRRP funds from the Department of Agriculture per Fund Utilization Report submitted to COA and DA on February 18, 2019 and March 12, 2019, respectively, in order to correct the overstatement of the Due to NGAs account reported in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The accounting entry to record the liquidation of the completed YRRP projects was made on March 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: Further, we recommended that henceforth, all FURs prepared and submitted to COA and the source agency of the fund shall be recorded immediately in the books to afford fair presentation of the Liabilities accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Recording of utilization is made upon preparation and submission of FURs to COA and source agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should record the Motor Vehicle in the books based on the documents from the PGO Leyte.
observations.managementAction: The journal entry to record the donated vehicle was already made on March 22, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the General Services Officer submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with the Acknowledgment Receipts of the donations-in kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, not necessarily in the required form, and the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any.
observations.managementAction: The documents regarding the donations received as well as their distribution was already submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: For all donations received in the future, we recommended that the General Services Officer should account and report these in a Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with the Acknowledgment Receipts of the donations-in kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, not necessarily in the required form, and the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

We recommended that the General Services Officer should conduct a physical count of inventories on hand as of April 19, 2021 and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and the RSMI together with the RIS for the months of January to April 16, 2021. The total amount of Inventories in the RPCI plus the issuances, minus purchases during the period shall be restored back in the books to reflect the correct balance of Inventories as of January 1, 2021.
observations.managementAction: The RIS for the inventories recorded as expense were already submitted and the report of physical count of inventories were already prepared in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Officer should submit the lacking supporting documents to avoid disallowance in audit and moving forward, he should ensure the submission of complete supporting documents for every transaction to establish propriety and validity.
observations.managementAction: The lacking supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team and were found valid and proper.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit written justification for the payment of the transactions even without the required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Justification was already submitted, and the Municipal Mayor gave assurance of full compliance thereafter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the BAC Chairman should submit a justification for the Audit Team ‘s evaluation why the implementation of supplemental feeding using CY 2018 funds from DSWD did not adhere to DSWD Administrative Order No. 04, series of 2010 or the Amended Guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program.
observations.managementAction: Justifications were already submitted to the Audit Team and were found sufficient and valid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculture Officer to submit the computation of the honoraria of P30,000.00 paid to the resource person of the seminar in order to determine whether or not the guidelines per DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-1 was followed.
observations.managementAction: The Notice of Disallowance pertaining to the payments made was already settled through payment by management.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agriculture Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit legal basis for granting honoraria to the participants of the seminar and incentive to the resource person to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Notice of Disallowance pertaining to the payments made was already settled through payment by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should make immediately the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors noted and reflect the correct RPT shares of the barangays and prepare the accompanying Disbursement Vouchers for release of the funds in consonance with the regulations.
observations.managementAction: The journal entries to adjust the over-recording of barangay shares on RPT amounting P233,987.92 and P53,152.94 were made on March 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer issue checks in favor of the concerned barangays based on the DV prepared in order to remit the RPT shares of the barangays.
observations.managementAction: Remittances of RPT shares of the barangays were already made in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman should require all contractors of LGU projects to provide the sufficient validity period of performance bonds posted to cover one year from project completion or up to issuance of final acceptance of the project by the LGU as prescribed in Section 39 of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Projects of the LGU paid in CY 2021 were already supported with performance securities to cover one year from project completion or up to issuance of final acceptance of the project by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should compute and deduct the corresponding taxes due from the account of each supplier/provider of goods and services which are subject to withholding and remit these to the BIR promptly.
observations.managementAction: Taxes for the payments of diesel, gasoline and lubricants were already withheld by the municipality.
