observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare an accounting entry to record the liquidation of the completed projects implemented out of YRRP funds from the Department of Agriculture per Fund Utilization Report submitted to COA and DA on February 18, 2019 and March 12, 2019, respectively, in order to correct the overstatement of the Due to NGAs account reported in the financial statements.
We also recommended for the Municipal Accountant to trace the amount of discrepancy of P2,740,340.60 so correcting entry could be immediately made.
Further, we recommended that henceforth, all FURs prepared and submitted to COA and the source agency of the fund shall be recorded immediately in the books to afford fair presentation of the Liabilities accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should instruct the General Services Officer to submit to the Audit Team for review and validation a Report on the Physical Count of Inventories to support the assertion that 5,263 bags of rice still remained at year end or, a Distribution List duly signed by the recipient beneficiaries. If both RPCI and Distribution List could not be submitted after ten days from receipt of this AOM, a Notice of Disallowance shall be issued by the Audit Team to the persons liable for the transaction.
For similar transactions in the future, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor should issue a clear instruction to an Officer who shall be responsible and accountable for the transaction to pinpoint accountability and ensure that government resources are safeguarded against wastage.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should record the Motor Vehicle in the books based on the documents from the PGO Leyte.
We also recommended that the General Services Officer submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with the Acknowledgment Receipts of the donations-in kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, not necessarily in the required form, and the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any.
For all donations received in the future, we recommended that the General Services Officer should account and report these in a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances supported with the Acknowledgment Receipts of the donations-in kind, proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries, not necessarily in the required form, and the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

We recommended that the General Services Officer should conduct a physical count of inventories on hand as of April 19, 2021 and submit the RPCI to the Municipal Accountant and the RSMI together with the RIS for the months of January to April 16, 2021. The total amount of Inventories in the RPCI plus the issuances, minus purchases during the period shall be restored back in the books to reflect the correct balance of Inventories as of January 1, 2021.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare journal entries to record inventory issuances only on the basis of a duly accomplished RSMI with supporting RIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should submit a copy of the Appropriation Ordinance authorizing the disbursements of P16,634,402.00 for COVID-19 programs out of the CY 2020 20% Development Fund of the LGU.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the Local Sanggunian and all department heads should strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 7160, Administrative Order No. 47 dated April 12, 1993 and PD 1445 on the budgetary requirements for the proper disposition of public funds.

We recommended that the MDRRM Officer should submit the lacking supporting documents to avoid disallowance in audit and moving forward, he should ensure the submission of complete supporting documents for every transaction to establish propriety and validity.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit written justification for the payment of the transactions even without the required supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer and the BAC Chairman should submit a justification for the Audit Team ‘s evaluation why the implementation of supplemental feeding using CY 2018 funds from DSWD did not adhere to DSWD Administrative Order No. 04, series of 2010 or the Amended Guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculture Officer to submit the computation of the honoraria of P30,000.00 paid to the resource person of the seminar in order to determine whether or not the guidelines per DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-1 was followed.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agriculture Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit legal basis for granting honoraria to the participants of the seminar and incentive to the resource person to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the GF and prepare all the required financial reports for the four (4) economic enterprise of the Municipality for monitoring of the operating performance and viability of the LEEs and to serve as a decision making tool of the agency. Transactions pertaining to an economic enterprise should be recorded in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
We also recommended that the LEE Managers (personnel in-charge) prepare the required 5-year business plan for each LEE.
We also recommended that the Municipal Finance Committee conduct the required Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review, and submit and present the same to the Municipal Mayor and local sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should make immediately the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors noted and reflect the correct RPT shares of the barangays and prepare the accompanying Disbursement Vouchers for release of the funds in consonance with the regulations.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer issue checks in favor of the concerned barangays based on the DV prepared in order to remit the RPT shares of the barangays.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should discontinue designating Job Order personnel as Collectors for the reason that they are not eligible for a fidelity bond with the Bureau of Treasury.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that all personnel in possession with funds and property owned by the Municipality should be a permanent employee and properly bonded in accordance with the provisions of the law.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman should require all contractors of LGU projects to provide the sufficient validity period of performance bonds posted to cover one year from project completion or up to issuance of final acceptance of the project by the LGU as prescribed in Section 39 of RA 9184.
We recommended further that the BAC should examine thoroughly the data contained in the Performance Securities and all other bidding documents of the Contracts so that validity of documents attached to it can be assured of and avoid invalidation of the Contract Agreement.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should compute and deduct the corresponding taxes due from the account of each supplier/provider of goods and services which are subject to withholding and remit these to the BIR promptly.
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor:
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
submit the Taxpayer’s Index Card to the Municipal Accountant at the start of each year as basis in recording RPT Receivables;
observations.managementAction: Submitted the Taxpayer’s Index Card to the Municipal Accountant

observations.recommendation: actively implement tax collection methods and strategies to increase RPT collections and resolve the bloating balances of uncollected RPT in the books;
observations.managementAction: Imposed 20% discount for early payment, conducted tax campaign to barangays and sent notice of delinquencies.

observations.recommendation: prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year including reasons for such non-collection, and;
observations.managementAction: Prepared and submitted to the Provincial Treasurer top 50 delinquent tax payers in order to apply remedies for the collection of Real Property Tax.

observations.recommendation: We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant record every January of each year the full amount of RPT due and collectible as receivables based on the computed amount per RPT Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index cards.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared a JEV to set up RPT receivable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
record correctly the collections from payments of Real Property Taxes, in accordance with the PPSAS prescribed accounting entries, and;
observations.managementAction: The collection on RPT tax was recorded properly.

observations.recommendation: prepare the necessary accounting entries to adjust the understatement of Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivable accounts and other related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has prepared adjusting entries for erroneous charging of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Billing personnel of WASH  and the Municipal Treasurer:
furnish the Municipal Accountant with the monthly Billing Statements of stall renters and water consumers so that the pertinent Receivables and Income will be recorded in the books of the LGU;
observations.managementAction: The Billing Clerk furnished monthly billing statement of stall renter and water consumers to the office of the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: intensify and prioritize the collection of all overdue receivables from stall tenants and water consumers to increase the income of the LGU for the maintenance of the stalls and its water system.
observations.managementAction: Send demand letters to stall tenants and water consumers for 1 month delayed payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the receivables in the books once copies of billing statements are received so that the actual income and receivable accounts can be reported in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant Recorded the receivable in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate each LGU Officials responsible in the accounting and reporting of the local road network of the LGU follow strictly the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008, monitor their compliance thereon and submit the correct reports at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has mandated the LGU officials responsible pursuant to the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We also reiterated the recommendations in previous year’s Annual Audit Report and in addition, we recommended that:
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components of road projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has provided the Municipal Accountant and the GSO in-charge with the description of the road components of the road network.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each component of the local road network.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has maintained ledger cards for each component of the Local Road Network although it has to be updated regularly.

observations.recommendation: The General Services Office complete Property Cards with the necessary data.
observations.managementAction: Prepared complete property cards for local road network for updating.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer accomplish the following:
complete the conduct of inventory of the LGU’s local roads;
observations.managementAction: Conducted the inventory of LGU’s local roads.

observations.recommendation: prepare an accurate Inventory of Local Roads, and;
observations.managementAction: Prepared an accurate Inventory of Local Roads.

observations.recommendation: submit the Inventory of Local Roads to DILG immediately and every three years thereafter, without need for demand from the said Office.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor confirm the compliance by the Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-52 and RA No. 7160 relative to the above-enumerated specific recommendations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor confirmed the compliance by the Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-52 and RA No. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group (TWG) scrutinize carefully the eligibility requirements of each bidder pursuant to “Annex G” of the IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Scrutinized the eligibility requirement of each bidder.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the BAC and TWG submit justification as to why an ineligible bidder was awarded the contract for the Design and Build – 3 Categories Adventure Park Facilities.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and TWG submitted a justification and committed to implement recommendations in their future transactions.

observations.recommendation: Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit justification why the Notice To Proceed was issued two months after the approval of the contract.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor submitted a justification and committed to implement the recommendations in their future transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justifications why Circle Z Ventures and Development Corporation was awarded with three new contracts despite non-disclosure in its eligibility documents all previously awarded contracts.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted a justification and committed to implement the audit recommendations in their future transactions.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the BAC scrutinize carefully the eligibility documents submitted by interested bidders so that any misrepresentations will be detected and to declare as eligible only those who strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC scrutinized carefully the eligibility requirements for each bidder .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and its Secretariat: (1) submit justifications on the enumerated deficiencies, and; (2) follow strictly the provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid legal sanctions.
observations.managementAction: The BAC and its secretariat submitted a justification.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor fast-track the procurement of the unimplemented programs, otherwise, evaluate its viability and return the funds to the source agency if found not feasible or if the implementation cannot be undertaken by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of the projects were done, but due to the COVID 19 threat, some projects in CY 2020 were not realized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance one year from project completion and to require contractors with completed projects to submit warranty security with sufficient validity period as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2. of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Final Acceptance of projects were now made 1 year after project completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, and members of the  Local Development Council, include only in the AIP and utilize the 20% DF, for  socio-economic and environmental development projects which are listed in Item 3 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-01 to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Only eligible projects were originally appropriated in the 20% DF in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: Also, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer re-align the fund appropriated for Construction of New Motorpool at Sitio Paraiso to other eligible projects.
observations.managementAction: The project was realigned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant re-compute at yearend the appropriations for LDRRM fund based on the actual total income from regular sources to determine the actual unexpended balance to be transferred to the special trust fund in the Trust Fund books.
observations.managementAction: Transferred the remaining unexpended DRRM fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee comply strictly with the provisions of RA 9184 with emphasis on eligibility screening of prospective bidders to avoid legal sanctions.
observations.managementAction: Complied strictly with the provisions of RA 9184 on eligibility screening of prospective bidders.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor fast-track the procurement of the unimplemented programs, otherwise, evaluate its viability and return the funds to the source agency if found not feasible or if the implementation cannot be undertaken by the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of the projects were done, but due to the COVID 19 threat, some projects in CY 2020 were not realized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant deposit immediately the unutilized amount to the Bureau of Treasury and submit the Report of Disbursement or Liquidation Report to the source agencies.  Strictly observe the provisions of the MOA, especially on immediate submission of liquidation reports and the return of unutilized balances.
observations.managementAction: Submitted FUR to the source agencies and returned the unutilized balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the members of the Sanggunian Bayan:
create other revenue-generating activities to improve the Park’s income, otherwise, reduce the expense for wages of JOs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has maintained special account in the books of the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
revert to the Due to BIR account the amount reclassified as Other Payables and Prior Year’s Adjustment;
observations.managementAction: Reverted and reclassified to Due to BIR account.

observations.recommendation: identify the persons/entities to whom the amount was deducted;
observations.managementAction: Identified the entities that were deducted by the said amount.

observations.recommendation: remit the amount to the BIR immediately;
observations.managementAction: Remitted to the BIR

observations.recommendation: reconcile monthly all taxes withheld from taxpayers and the amount remitted to the BIR to avoid penalties.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was done by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System headed by the Municipal Mayor:

Require all Department Heads to submit their respective accomplishments on GAD related programs, projects and activities including the equivalent amount of expenditures to ensure that the LGU complied with the minimum requirement of GAD related expenditures to at least 5% of the total estimated income of the Municipality;
observations.managementAction: Regular monthly meeting of Execom and TWG and submitted Monthly Status Report of Implementation.

observations.recommendation: Submit the necessary documents to support the LGU’s annual GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: Submitted annual accomplishment report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Limit the hiring of Job Order workers to indispensable functions only and pay the prescribed daily minimum wage as prescribed by the Regional Tripartite Wages and Productivity Boards for Region VIII;
observations.managementAction: No job order were subsequently were who reached compulsory retirement age.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from hiring Job Order Workers who have reached the compulsory retirement age;
observations.managementAction: No job order were subsequently were who reached compulsory retirement age.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Agriculture Officer:
justify the excessive purchase of bangus feeds which were not distributed and have expired;
observations.managementAction: Justification were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: adhere to the regulations on procuring goods equivalent only to three-month supply and;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist office now adheres to the 3-month supply of goods.

observations.recommendation: discontinue the distribution of the expired Bangus feeds to avoid adverse effects on the cultured bangus of the fisherfolks association.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist discontinued the distribution of expired Bangus Feeds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with the limitations on the grant of uniform/clothing allowance as stated in Budget Circular No. 2018-1.
observations.managementAction: No clothing allowance was granted to the Job Order personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and TWG review thoroughly and with utmost care all bidding documents before any contract is awarded to ensure that the eligibility requirements prescribed in Republic Act No. 9184 are duly verified.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents on the dormant receivable accounts with evidences that the same could no longer be collected.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for write-off based on the documents submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

We also recommended that the BAC scrutinize carefully the eligibility documents submitted by interested bidders so that any misrepresentations will be detected and to declare as eligible only those who strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.reason: the BAC still do not carefully scrutinize the documents submitted to them by the bidders.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Billing personnel of WASH  and the Municipal Treasurer:
furnish the Municipal Accountant with the monthly Billing Statements of stall renters and water consumers so that the pertinent Receivables and Income will be recorded in the books of the LGU;
intensify and prioritize the collection of all overdue receivables from stall tenants and water consumers to increase the income of the LGU for the maintenance of the stalls and its water system.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the receivables in the books once copies of billing statements are received so that the actual income and receivable accounts can be reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor mandate each LGU Officials responsible in the accounting and reporting of the local road network of the LGU follow strictly the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2015-008, monitor their compliance thereon and submit the correct reports at year-end.
We also reiterated the recommendations in previous year’s Annual Audit Report and in addition, we recommended that:
The Municipal Assessor determine the cost of the road lot component for each road project of the LGU for recognition in the financial statements;
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components of road projects.
The Municipal Accountant maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each component of the local road network.
The General Services Office complete Property Cards with the necessary data.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group (TWG) scrutinize carefully the eligibility requirements of each bidder pursuant to “Annex G” of the IRR of RA 9184.
We also recommended that the BAC and TWG submit justification as to why an ineligible bidder was awarded the contract for the Design and Build – 3 Categories Adventure Park Facilities.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit justification why the Notice To Proceed was issued two months after the approval of the contract.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justifications why Circle Z Ventures and Development Corporation was awarded with three new contracts despite non-disclosure in its eligibility documents all previously awarded contracts.
We also recommended that the BAC scrutinize carefully the eligibility documents submitted by interested bidders so that any misrepresentations will be detected and to declare as eligible only those who strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184.

We recommended that the BAC and its Secretariat: (1) submit justifications on the enumerated deficiencies, and; (2) follow strictly the provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid legal sanctions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Engineer issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance one year from project completion and to require contractors with completed projects to submit warranty security with sufficient validity period as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2. of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the LGU officials concerned impose liquidated damages on Toyota Tacloban, Leyte, Inc. in the amount of P107,726.60 , pursuant to Item 3.1 of Annex “D” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, and members of the  Local Development Council, include only in the AIP and utilize the 20% DF, for  socio-economic and environmental development projects which are listed in Item 3 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 to avoid audit disallowances.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer re-align the fund appropriated for Construction of New Motorpool at Sitio Paraiso to other eligible projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant re-compute at yearend the appropriations for LDRRM fund based on the actual total income from regular sources to determine the actual unexpended balance to be transferred to the special trust fund in the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee comply strictly with the provisions of RA 9184 with emphasis on eligibility screening of prospective bidders to avoid legal sanctions.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor fast-track the procurement of the unimplemented programs, otherwise, evaluate its viability and return the funds to the source agency if found not feasible or if the implementation cannot be undertaken by the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant deposit immediately the unutilized amount to the Bureau of Treasury and submit the Report of Disbursement or Liquidation Report to the source agencies.  Strictly observe the provisions of the MOA, especially on immediate submission of liquidation reports and the return of unutilized balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the members of the Sanggunian Bayan:
require the Local Finance Committee  to review  the operations of Palompon Eco-Adventure Park and render a report thereon; and
use the said report to evaluate the viability of said enterprise;
create other revenue-generating activities to improve the Park’s income, otherwise, reduce the expense for wages of JOs.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  maintain Special Accounts in the books of the General Fund of the LGU for each local economic enterprise and prepare the required financial reports for monitoring of its operating performance and for decision-making of the policy makers of the LGU.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with the limitations on the grant of uniform/clothing allowance as stated in Budget Circular No. 2018-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the General Services Office to prepare and submit the required reports monthly to the Office of the COA Audit Team thru the Municipal Accountant, in accordance with regulations.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Internal Audit Unit in the LGU to handle internal control functions within the agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Agriculturist, being the person with full knowledge on the recipients of the agricultural materials and supplies from YRRP, submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) supported with the RIS signed by the recipient barangays;
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist submitted to the Municipal Accountant  the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) supported with RIS signed by the recipient barangays.

observations.recommendation: the General Services Officer, issue and submit to the Municipal Accountant, a certification that the construction materials were already distributed to the recipient barangays, supported with a certification or RIS signed by the recipient barangays based on the records of the GSO;
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer submitted to the Municipal Accountant a certification that the construction and materials were distributed to the recipient barangays supported with RIS signed by the recipient barangays.

observations.recommendation: the Municipal Accountant record in the appropriate Inventory accounts the issued agricultural supplies and construction materials based on the SSMI of the Municipal Agriculturist and the General Services Officer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, the MDRRM Officer, the Municipal Budget Officer, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit justifications why disbursements totalling P2,492,980.41 should not be disallowed in audit, for using the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on expenditures which were not in accordance with its intended purposes.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM officer submitted a justification to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, and other officials concerned, utilize the LDRRMF solely and strictly, for disaster-related activities as mandated in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 20131.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adhered strictly with the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 20131.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: Submit Fund Utilization Reports to the source agencies for completed projects/programs;
observations.managementAction: There were unused Fund Transfers from NGAs which were not yet used nor returned to the source agencies.

observations.recommendation: Write a formal inquiry to the Bureau of Fishery and Aquatic Resources and Department of Agriculture whether the LGU has an existing payable to the said NGAs;
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is now in the process of writing a formal inquiry to the Bureau of Fishery and Aquatic Resources and Department of Agriculture.

observations.recommendation: Return all unused funds of the NGAs on completed projects, and;
observations.managementAction: The Municipality returned some of the unused funds.

observations.recommendation: Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for each fund transfer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant now have separate subsidiary ledgers for each fund transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Agriculturist, the representative of Circle Z Ventures & Dev. Corp, other signatories of the certificate of completion and certificate of inspection, and other officials involved in the implementation of the project on Construction of Goat House – Tinago submit justification as to why the documents they signed contained false information and why there were inconsistencies in the recipients of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Muncipal Agriculturist and the Municipal Engineer submitted a justification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer supervise and monitor the implementation of the barangay projects funded from the 20% Development Fund of the LGU.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Barangay Officials concerned to submit immediately all Liquidation Reports pertaining to funds transferred by the LGU, otherwise, no subsequent fund should be released to the said barangay.
observations.managementAction: Some fund transfers were liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the funds transferred to barangays in CY 2018 and utilize the computer application/software in recording financial transactions to ensure efficient and accurate accounting process.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still on the process of recording fund transfers to all barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disbursing Officer: (1) obtain cash advances equal to the net amount of monthly payrolls;
observations.managementAction: The disbursing officer now obtain cash advances equal to the net amount of monthly payrolls.

observations.recommendation: (2) return any excess/unused amount to the account of the LGU, and;
observations.managementAction: Any excess amount of cash advances were returned to the LGU.

observations.recommendation: (3) use cash advances only for its specific purposes.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances were now used only for its specific purpose.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer process the cash advances of the DO only when supported with a payroll, the net amount of which is equal to the amount of cash advance being granted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer now grants cash advances to the disbursing officer equivalent to the net amount of payroll.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRM Officer ensure that the LDRRMF is spent solely for disaster-related activities as mandated in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 20131 and as reflected in the LGU’s LDRRMF Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development in order to promote and protect child care and development.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board has incorporated in its budget programs and activities intended for ECCD.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justifications to the Audit Team on their deviations and Statements All On-going and Completed Projects are found in order.
We also recommended that the BAC scrutinize carefully the eligibility documents submitted by interested bidders so that any misrepresentations will be detected and to declare as eligible only those who strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted a justification to the Audit Team on their deviations and Statements All On-going and Completed Projects are found in order.
observations.reason: Failure to adhere to provisions of RA 9184

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in its budget programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development in order to promote and protect child care and development.
observations.managementAction: No budget for ECCD was incorporated in the Local School Board Budget.
observations.reason: No reasons given

observations.recommendation:
1.10  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant:  (1)  identify  the
specific public infrastructures acquired prior to CY 2015;  (2)  record  the
same  to  the  appropriate  Infrastructure  Asset  accounts;  (3)   maintain
property ledger cards for each public infrastructure,  and;  (4)  prepare  a
lapsing schedule to compute depreciation of each asset.

2.9 We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  reclassify  the  road
projects totaling P63,062,028.30 from Other Infrastructures account to  Road
Networks account.

2.10 We also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Engineer  and  the  General
Services Officer conduct of inventory of all  local  road  projects  of  the
LGU,  prepare a report thereon  and  reconcile  the  Inventory  Report  with
those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure  of  the  Accounting
Office.

3.12 We recommended that:


    3.12.1  The  Municipal  Agriculturist,  being  the  person  with   full
    knowledge on the recipients of the agricultural materials and  supplies
    from YRRP, submit to the Municipal Accountant the Summary  of  Supplies
    and Materials Issued (SSMI)  supported  with  the  RIS  signed  by  the
    recipient Associations;


    3.12.2 The General Services Officer, issue and submit to the  Municipal
    Accountant,  a  certification  that  the  construction  materials  were
    already distributed  to  the  recipient  barangays,  supported  with  a
    certification or RIS signed by the recipient  barangays  based  on  the
    records of the GSO;


    3.12.3 The Municipal Accountant record  in  the  appropriate  Inventory
    accounts the issued agricultural supplies  and  construction  materials
    based on the SSMI  of  the  Municipal  Agriculturist  and  the  General
    Services Officer.

4.19 We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor,  the  MDRRM  Officer,  the
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal  Accountant  utilize  the  LDRRMF
solely and strictly, for disaster-related  activities  as  mandated  in  the
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No.  20131,  to  avoid  disallowances  in  audit.   This
recommendation  is  without  prejudice  to  the  issuance   of   Notice   of
Disallowance, if deemed warranted.

5.8  We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant:  (1)   submit   Fund
Utilization Reports to the source agencies for completed  projects/programs;
(2) Write a formal inquiry to the Bureau of Fishery  and  Aquatic  Resources
and Department of Agriculture whether the LGU has  an  existing  payable  to
the said NGAs; (3)  Return  all  unused  funds  of  the  NGAs  on  completed
projects, and; (4)  maintain  separate  subsidiary  ledgers  for  each  fund
transfer.

6.13 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer,  Municipal
Agriculturist and other LGU personnel responsible in the  implementation  of
projects/programs, especially those sourced from NGAs: (1)  follow  strictly
all provisions in the Memorandum  of  Agreement  and  the  approved  project
proposals; (2) submit documents  relative  to  the  projects/programs  which
contain true and correct information and conforms with the MOA;  (3)  obtain
written approval from the funding agency for any  deviation  or  changes  in
the project implementation, and;  (4)  conduct  actual  inspection  of   the
project especially its completion phase. These  recommendations  is  without
prejudice  to  the  issuance  of  Notice  of  Disallowance   on   the   said
transaction.

7.7 We recommended that the BAC and TWG review thoroughly  and  with  utmost
care all bidding documents before any contract is  awarded  to  ensure  that
the eligibility requirements prescribed in Republic Act No.  9184  are  duly
verified.

8.9 We recommended that:

   a.) The Municipal Mayor require  the  Barangay  Officials  concerned  to
       submit  immediately  all  Liquidation  Reports  pertaining  to  funds
       transferred by the LGU,  otherwise,  no  subsequent  fund  should  be
       released to the said barangay.
   b.) The Municipal Engineer supervise and monitor the  implementation  of
       barangay projects funded from the 20% Development Fund of the LGU;
   c.) The Municipal Accountant record the funds transferred  to  barangays
       in CY 2018 and utilize the computer application/software in recording
       financial transactions to ensure efficient  and  accurate  accounting
       process.

9.6 We recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  the  necessary
documents on the dormant receivable accounts with evidences that  the  same
could no longer be collected.

9.7 We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor file the request for write-
off based on the documents submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

10.5 We recommended that the Disbursing Officer: (1)  obtain  cash  advances
equal to the net amount of monthly payrolls; (2)  return  any  excess/unused
amount to the account of the LGU, and; (3) use cash advances  only  for  its
specific purposes.

10.6 We  also  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  Treasurer
process the cash advances of the DO only when supported with a payroll,  the
net amount of which is equal to the amount of cash advance being granted.

To address the issue on delayed recording of check disbursements, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of releasing DVs to payees and require them to sign the DVs in the designated area within premises of the Municipal Treasurer’s office.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the RCI for checks actually released to payees, make a “List of Unreleased Checks” for unclaimed checks to be attached to the RCI and submit on a daily basis the RCI to the Accounting Office for recording and JEV preparation.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer has stopped the practice of releasing DVs and other documents to the payees. There were no unrecorded checks issued during the year.
The Municipal Treasurer prepared the RCI for checks actually released to the payees and attached the  List of Unreleased Checks.

The Municipal Accountant determined the names of the Municipal Employees whose loan amortizations were paid by the LGU and submitted the same to the Audit Team.
The Municipality has designated separate personnel for the preparation of GSIS loan remittances and the payroll preparer.
The Municipal Accountant review at all times, all remittances to GSIS against payroll deductions before processing the transactions; the Human Resource and Management Officer to reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium contributions; the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to furnish the payroll preparer with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted in the payroll based on the said GSIS billing.
We strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor require each concerned employee to refund the amount paid to GSIS by the LGU for their personal loans without payroll deductions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the 20% of IRA be appropriated for projects with sufficient funding and not merely on reliance with assistance from other agencies in order for optimal utilization of the 20% development fund.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2018, the utilization of the 20% Development Fund was already maximized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer submit justification why the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund totaling P220,000.00 was utilized for the project Installation of CCTV which is not a disaster-related project and not in accordance with the intended purpose of the fund. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRM Officer ensure that the LDRRMF is spent solely for disaster-related activities as mandated in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 20131 and as reflected in the LGU’s LDRRMF Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRM Officer justified the utilization of the LDRRM Fund for the procurement of CCTV.
observations.reason: Justificati ons were presented by the LGU personnel concerned

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRMO refrain from using the Quick Response Fund unless there is a declaration of a state of calamity by proper authorities so as not to deplete the said standby fund.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payments out of the LDRRMF only when it is specified in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.managementAction:
There was no disbursement out of the Quick Response fund for CY 2018.
All disbursements out of the LDRRMF is specified in the LDRRMFIP.

observations.recommendation: Require each Job Order to sign a Contract of Service which will indicate the basic and vital data required in any contract agreement including the project/program or office which needs the services of a Job Order; assign a specific personnel to maintain a database of all Job Order personnel hired by the Municipal Government to include personal information of each JO which are relevant to their job assignment; evaluate the necessity of the JOs to warrant year-round appointments; To preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments of JO wages are supported with the Contracts of Service of each JO and Accomplishment Reports certified correct by the official who has primary knowledge of the work performed in order to validate actual services rendered.
observations.managementAction:
Hiring of JO personnel were supported with Contract of Service.
A personnel is assigned to maintain a database of all Job Order Personnel.
The HRMO evaluated the necessity of the services of the JOs.
The Municipal Accountant scrutinize the attachments on the payment of wages of the JOs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the General Services Officer furnish the Municipal Accountant with the documents pertaining to the disposed properties.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the properties in the books of the LGU and record the loss or gain on its sale.
observations.managementAction:
The General Services Officer submitted to the Municipal Accountant documents pertaining to the disposed properties.
The Municipal Accountant has de-recognize in the books the disposed properties of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall require the Municipal Engineer to monitor compliance by all contractors of the installation of project billboards in all projects implemented by the LGU on the installation of project billboards in all projects pursuant to Paragraph 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The contractors complied with the installation of project billboards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer require each concerned end-users or implementing units of the municipality, to indicate in their respective PPMPs all the required information for the procurement and implementation of the various programs, projects and activities for the year. The same information should support the data reflected in the approved Annual Budget and Annual Investment Plan of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: All the required information was incorporated in the PPMP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the GF and prepare all the required financial reports for the four (4) economic enterprise of the Municipality for monitoring of the operating performance and viability of the LEEs and to serve as a decision making tool of the agency.
Transactions pertaining to an economic enterprise should be recorded in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant now maintains separate subsidiary ledgers for all 4 economic enterprise of the LGU however no separate FS were prepared for eah.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Officer submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF within the reglamentary period as required per COA Circular 2012-002 to enable the Audit Team and other concerned offices to conduct evaluation of the LDRRMF utilization on time.
observations.managementAction: The Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF were only submitted to COA during audit.
observations.reason: They forgot to submit the reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the submission of the lacking documents to prove that: (a) the purchased lot was free from any encumbrances; (b) the payee was the rightful owner of the purchased lot; (c) the sale was duly registered with the Register of Deeds. Likewise, require the Provincial Appraisal Committee to submit the BIR Zonal Valuation to support the established purchased price.
observations.managementAction: The required lacking documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
To address the issue on delayed recording of check disbursements, we
recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of releasing DVs to
payees and require them to sign the DVs in the designated area within the premises
of the Municipal Treasurer’s office.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the RCI for
checks actually issued, prepare a List of Unreleased Checks for unclaimed checks
and the List of Cancelled Checks, and submit both lists together with the RCI on a
daily or weekly basis to the Accounting Office for recording and JEV preparation.

We recommended that the General Services Officer furnish the Municipal
Accountant with the documents pertaining to the properties which were disposed
through sale. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the properties in the books of the LGU and
record the loss or gain on its sale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
4. identify the employees whose loan amortizations were paid by the LGU
without the corresponding deductions in their salaries, and submit the list
to COA;
5. install strong internal control procedures within her Office and assign a
separate personnel for the preparation of GSIS loan remittances from the
payroll preparer;
6. review, at all times, all remittances to GSIS against payroll deductions
before processing the transactions.

We also recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer
reconcile the LGU records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said
agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the
records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium contributions.
Also, the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to furnish monthly all payroll preparer
with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the correct amount of monthly
premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted in the payroll based on
the said GSIS billing

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all employees
concerned to refund the entire amount of their loan amortizations remitted to the
GSIS using LGU funds.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit
justifications why LJPM Builders and Construction Supply was awarded with nine
new contracts despite non-disclosure in its eligibility documents all previously
awarded contracts.

We also recommended that the BAC scrutinize carefully the eligibility
documents submitted by interested bidders so that any misrepresentations will be
detected and to declare as eligible only those who strictly comply with the provisions
of RA 9184.

We recommended that that the 20% of IRA be appropriated for projects
with sufficient funding and not merely on reliance with assistance from other
agencies in order for optimal utilization of the 20% development fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant and the MDRRM Officer submit justification why the
LDRRMF was utilized for the project Installation of CCTV which is not a disaster-
related project and not in accordance with the intended purpose of the fund.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRM
Officer ensure that the LDRRMF is spent solely for disaster- activities as mandated
in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 20131 and as reflected in the LGU’s
LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRMO refrain
from using the Quick Response Fund unless there is a declaration of a state of
calamity by proper authorities so as not to deplete the said standby fund. We also
recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payments out of the
LDRRMF only when it is specified in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertakes the following
corrective measures:
1.) Require each Job Order to sign a Contract of Service which will indicate the
basic and vital data required in any contract agreement including the
project/program or office which needs the services of a Job Order;
2.) assign a specific personnel to maintain a database of all Job Order personnel
hired by the Municipal Government to include personal information of each
JO which are relevant to their job assignment;
3.) evaluate the necessity of the services of the JOs to warrant year-round
appointments;
To preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit, we also
recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payments of JO wages are
supported with the Contracts of Service of each JO and Accomplishment Reports
certified correct by the official who has primary knowledge of the work performed in
order to validate actual services rendered.

We recommended, and management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Engineer to monitor compliance by all contractors of the
projects implemented by the LGU on the installation of project billboards in all
projects sites.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer require each end-user
or implementing units of the municipality, to indicate in their respective PPMPs all
the required information for the procurement and implementation of the various
programs projects and activities for the year. The same information should
support the data reflected in the approved Annual Budget and Annual Investment
Plan of the municipality.

We recommended that the Local School Board incorporate in its budget
programs and activities intended for Early Childhood Care Development in order to
promote and protect child care and development.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain
Special Accounts in the GF and prepare all the required financial reports for the
four (4) economic enterprise of the Municipality for monitoring of the operating
performance and viability of the LEEs and to serve as a decision making tool of the
agency. Transactions pertaining to an economic enterprise should be recorded in the
corresponding subsidiary ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate adjusting entries to record the Property, Plant and Equipment which were closed in the Trust Fund books and correct the accumulated depreciation and reclassification of Property, Plant and Equipment.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 1 Journal Entry Voucher was prepared to draw the correcting entries for the overstatement/understatement of Property, Plant and Equipment. Accumulated Depreciation for the PPE’s affected were closed in the financial statements in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the understatement of Construction Materials Inventory and Other Structures accounts to show the correct balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 2 Journal Entry Voucher was prepared for classification of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor, as the Chairperson of the LPRAT, make representation with the appropriate source agencies to verify status of the BuB projects where no funds were downloaded and ensure that the required reports and documentations are duly complied with in order to expedite release of BuB funds. Also, the assigned BuB Focal Person to conduct regular project monitoring on the status of the projects and ensure that the implementation of the projects are in accordance with the guidelines and the procedures set forth in DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No.7
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 3 The BuB projects were already received and implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct all Barangay officials concerned to submit immediately all Liquidation Reports pertaining to funds transferred by the LGU for the implementation of various barangay programs and ensure that such funds are liquidated. We also recommend that proper supervision and monitoring be done by the LGU officials on the implementation of these barangay projects funded from the 20% Development Fund of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 4 Management policy Implemented "No Financial Assistance will be granted if the Barangay Treasurer will not prepare FUR and acknowledge the report by COA."

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the LGU officials implement the projects and activities identified and programmed during the year to be funded from the LDRRMF and include in the updated LDRRMIP the prior year’s unimplemented programs, projects and activities to ensure readiness of the LGU in the occurrence of disaster.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 & CY 2012 AAR Finding No. 5 The projects are already implemented but the utilization of the unexpended balance of the prior year’s unimplemented programs were not utilized immediately because of the long term Plan of the LGU to utilize it in the procurement of heavy equipment which are important to the DRRM program of the LGU.
observations.reason: The unexpended balances were not immediately utilized because of the long term Plan of the LGU to utilize it in the procurement of heavy equipment which are important to the DRRM program of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the MDRRM Officer submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the reglamentary period as required in COA Circular 2012-002 to enable the Audit Team and COA and other concerned offices to conduct evaluation of the LDRRMF utilization on time.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 6 The MRRM officer adhere on COAs recommendation to submit monthly Utilization report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF. However the monthly Utilization report were not regularly done due to lack of detailed personnel assigned at LDRRM office.
observations.reason: Monthly submission of the report were not regularly done due to lack of detailed personnel assigned at LDRRM office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant deduct the ten percent (10%) retention money from every progress payment of contractor of all infrastructure contracts and demand the contractor to submit surety bond for warranty security for undiscovered structural defects and failures of contractors and third party liabilities. The same must be substituted with an irrevocable standby letter of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bond if the project is on schedule and is satisfactorily undertaken.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 7 The 10% retention were already deducted for progress billing and the agency already demanded the contractor to submit surety bond for completed projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the Request for Quotations for procurement transactions through "shopping" and "small value procurement" with ABCs above P50,000.00 shall be posted in the PhilGEPS website to promote transparency and competitiveness in each procurement activity of the agency.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 8 The Management submission of the approved Annual Budget for the Contracts (ABCs) above P50,000.00 were already posted in the PhilGEPS.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the submission of the lacking documents to prove that: (a) the purchased lot was free from any encumbrances; (b) the payee was the rightful owner of the purchased lot; (c) the sale was duly registered with the Register of Deeds. Likewise, require the Provincial Appraisal Committee to submit the BIR Zonal Valuation to support the purchased price.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Finding No. 9 Management adhered on COAs recommendati on to submit the documents. The Management was able to facilitate the submission of the documents necessary to transfer legal ownership to the LGU. The documents were already submitted.
observations.reason: The Management was able to facilitate submission of documents; transfer of legal ownership to the LGU was still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the conduct of the physical inventory of all the properties of the LGU by the Inventory Committee and for the preparation of the Report of the Physical Inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment. Reconciliation between the Inventory Report and the accounting records should be conducted by the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee and for the former to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect an accurate balance of the PPE account.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 & CY 2012 AAR Finding No. 10 The Inventory Committee conduct the Physical Inventory and the report of such activities were submitted to the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Inventory Report with the accounting records. The accounting records were updated and necessary adjusting entries were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the utilization of the proceeds of the Special Education Fund be limited to the prioritized projects, programs, and activities as mandated in Sections 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160 and that the expenditures charged to SEF be limited to the elementary and secondary schools as mandated in the law.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR Finding No. 11 The Management adhered on COAs recommendation and the utilization of the proceeds of the Special Education Fund were limited to the prioritized projects, programs and activities as mandated in Sections 272 and 100 (c) of RA 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the submission of the written authority of the DOF recognizing PNB as an authorized depository bank of the LGU. In the absence thereof, we recommend that these bank accounts be closed in compliance with the NGAS Manual, Volume I, BSP Circular Letter dated March 14, 2006 and Department of Finance Order No. 27-05 dated December 9, 2005.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 & CY 2012 AAR Finding No. 2 Management submitted a written authority to DOF requesting for the continuity of PNB as an authorized depository bank of the LGU for it is accessible in depositing LGU daily collections.

observations.recommendation: To achieve on-time submission of accounts, we recommend that the Treasurer and Accountant be directed to promptly turn-over accounts, documents, reports and journals which shall be duly updated. Transmittal letters shall be used to prove compliance.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR Finding No. 8 Prompt submission of Municipal accounts were already been complied and the accounts already been updated and submitted for audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate
accounting entries to record the Property, Plant and Equipment which were closed
in the Trust Fund books but inadvertently not recorded in the General Fund and
correct the overstatement/understatement of the Accumulated Depreciation for the
corresponding PPEs affected to bring the balances of these accounts in the financial
statements in order.

During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatement of the Property, Plant and
Equipment account.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare appropriate adjusting
entries to correct the understatement of Construction Materials Inventory and
Other Structures accounts to show the correct balances in the financial statements.

During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant agreed to correct the
understatement of the accounts affected.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the LPRAT,
make representations with the appropriate source agencies to verify status of the
BuB projects where no funds were downloaded and ensure that the required reports
and documentations were duly complied with in order to expedite release of BuB
funds. Also, the assigned BuB Focal Person conduct regular project monitoring on
the status of the projects and ensure that the implementation of the projects are in
accordance with the guidelines and the procedures set forth in DBM-DILG-DSWD-
NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7.

The Municipal Administrator agreed to facilitate in the verification of the status of
the BUB projects without funds downloaded and ensure that the required documentations
shall be complied with to expedite release of BUB funds.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct all Barangay officials
concerned to submit immediately all Liquidation Reports pertaining to funds
transferred by the LGU for the implementation of various barangay programs and
projects most particularly those due for liquidation already.

We also recommend that proper supervision and monitoring be done by the LGU
officials on the implementation of these barangay projects funded from the 20%
Development Fund of the LGU.

The Municipal Accountant, during the exit conference, agreed to adhere to the audit
recommendation on enforcing the barangay officials to liquidate the previous funds
released to them before receiving additional funds from the LGU.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the LGU officials concerned
implement the projects and activities identified and programmed during the year to
be funded from the LDRRMF and include in the updated LDRRMIP the prior
years’ unimplemented programs, projects and activities to ensure readiness of the
LGU in the occurrence of disaster.

The Municipal Administrator and other LGU officials concerned agreed to adhere
to the recommendations.

We recommend that the MDRRM Officer submit the Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRMF within the reglementary period as required per COA
Circular 2012-002 to enable the Audit Team and other concerned offices to conduct
evaluation of the LDRRMF utilization on time.

The Municipal Administrator and Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the
Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on time.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant deduct the ten percent (10% )
retention money from every progress payment of contractors of all infrastructure
contracts as warranty security for undiscovered structural defects, failures of
contractors and third party liabilities. The same must be substituted with an
irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or
surety bonds callable on demand, if the project is on schedule and is satisfactorily
undertaken.

The Municipal Accountant admitted that there were projects not deducted of 10%
retention money, but, promised to secure surety bonds in lieu of the later..

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee of the municipality
ensure that all Request for Quotations for procurement transactions through
“shopping” and “small value procurement” with ABCs above P50,000.00 shall be
posted in the PhilGEPS website to promote transparency and competitiveness in
each procurement activity of the agency.

The Municipal Administrator, during the exit conference, agreed to follow the
audit recommendations.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to
facilitate the submission of the lacking documents to prove that: (a) the purchased
lot was free from any encumbrances; (b) the payee was the rightful owner of the
purchased lot; (c) the sale was duly registered with the Register of Deeds. Likewise,
require the Provincial Appraisal Committee to submit the BIR Zonal Valuation to
support the established purchase price.

In the exit conference, the Municipal Administrator agreed to submit the lacking
supporting documents to the Audit Team.
